steps.text: Seal the autoclaves to the wall they pass through.
Position the bioseal for equipment access and maintenance from outside the facility.
Ensure only one autoclave door can be opened at a time.
Complete the decontamination cycle before opening the outside door to the autoclave.
Decontaminate cages prior to removal from the cabinet.
Design the cage wash area to accommodate high-pressure spray systems and strong chemical disinfectants.
Document the animal facility design parameters and operational procedures.
Verify that the design and operational parameters are met before operation.
Retest the facility annually or after significant modifications.
Provide appropriate communication systems between the animal facility and the outside.
Develop and implement provisions for emergency communication and access or egress.

preconditions.text: In specific legal situations, an institution may exclude a worker who declines to receive a potentially protective licensed vaccine against a virulent pathogen strain from working directly with that agent.

constraints.text: Use appropriate methods and personal protective equipment (PPE), including dedicated laboratory clothing, heavy or chainmail gloves to avoid cuts or sticks from cutting instruments or bone fragments, and an N95 respirator combined with a face shield or a PAPR.
Ample coverage of a 10% bleach solution should be used during and after the procedure for decontamination of exposed or contaminated surfaces and equipment.
To prevent the generation of aerosols, a handsaw is recommended instead of an oscillating saw and contact of the saw with brain tissue is avoided.

Personnel working in pressurized suits should be trained in the strategic use of penning, gating, and animal restraint equipment to minimize potential contact with animals, animal waste, and sharp surfaces.
Administrative controls and policies should recommend a minimum of two workers to be present in the containment area at all times (i.e., a “buddy system”).
All staff should be trained on appropriate response procedures for time-sensitive emergencies involving workers who are pinned or otherwise trapped by equipment or animals.
PPE should be carefully selected to provide the appropriate level of protection needed without compromising the health of the laboratory professional or their ability to safely perform their duties.
